content: Neurotransmitter systems are chemical messengers that transmit signals across synapses in the brain. Dopamine regulates reward, motivation, and motor control; dysfunction is implicated in Parkinson's disease, schizophrenia, and addiction. Serotonin modulates mood, sleep, appetite, and cognition; SSRIs target this system for depression and anxiety. Norepinephrine affects arousal, attention, and stress response; SNRIs and NRIs target this pathway. Glutamate is the primary excitatory neurotransmitter, critical for learning and memory via NMDA and AMPA receptors; excess glutamate can cause excitotoxicity. GABA is the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter; benzodiazepines enhance GABA activity. Imbalances in these systems underlie many psychiatric disorders.
